name: Shared Project Permissions Triage
summary: Collect read-only evidence for shared project directory permission failures.
description: This skill helps HPC users and support teams diagnose permission denied failures in shared project, scratch, and collaboration directories without changing ownership, modes, ACLs, or group membership.

tools.name: namei
tools.notes: Useful on Linux systems for inspecting path traversal permissions.

tools.name: getfacl
tools.notes: Useful on filesystems that expose POSIX ACLs.

inputs.name: target_path
inputs.description: Shared file or directory that produced a permission denied, group access, or collaboration failure.

inputs.name: log_file
inputs.description: Optional job, transfer, or application log with permission-related errors.

outputs.name: permissions_triage_report
outputs.description: Read-only report with current identity, group membership, path metadata, traversal permissions, ACL output when available, and relevant log clues.

outputs.name: offline_permissions_review
outputs.description: Separated traversal, ACL-mask, and application permission signals from saved evidence.

examples.title: Review saved traversal, ACL, and log evidence

tests.description: Validate the manifest and example artifacts.
Collect read-only evidence for the current directory.
Require traversal, ACL mask, and application-denied signals in synthetic fixtures.
Run against a user-owned shared project test directory and confirm the script does not change ownership, modes, ACLs, group membership, or files.
